title: Oracle PeopleSoft ExecuteProcessActivityCommand External Control of File Path Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
description:
This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of Oracle PeopleSoft. Although authentication is required to exploit this vulnerability, the existing authentication mechanism can be bypassed.
The specific flaw exists within the ExecuteProcessActivityCommand class. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of a user-supplied path prior to using it in file operations. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of the service account.
addtionnal_details:
Oracle has issued an update to correct this vulnerability. More details can be found at:
https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/alert-cve-2026-35273.html
timeline:
2026-06-10 - Vulnerability reported to vendor
2026-06-24 - Coordinated public release of advisory
2026-06-24  - Advisory Updated
credit: Bobby Gould (@bobbygould5) and Minh Giang of TrendAI Zero Day Initiative, Lucas Miller of TrendAI Research
